summary:
Endothelin-1 plays a significant role in hemodynamic instability during hemodialysis by acting as a potent vasoconstrictor that elevates vascular resistance, especially under volume overload. Blocking endothelin receptor activity has been associated with improved blood pressure control and reduction in intradialytic hypotension or hypertension, confirming its clinical relevance.  

\boxed{A}

Only two sentences focused exclusively on key clinical points:

Endothelin-1 contributes to intradialytic hypertension by promoting vasoconstriction due to elevated vascular resistance in volume-overloaded states. Clinical trials demonstrating that endothelin receptor antagonists improve blood pressure stability during dialysis confirm its pathophysiological significance.
